name: Compromised barrier
dek: The condition produced by skincare itself, more often than by anything else. Six weeks of strict, boring repair will resolve almost every case — but the temptation to "add one active" too early is the reason most people cycle through the same problem twice a year.
oneliner: Over-exfoliated skin needs the boring stack for 8 weeks.

triggers.n: The cardinal sign. Means the stratum corneum is no longer intact.
TEWL has spiked. Hydration evaporates faster than it can be replaced.
Things that worked last month now sting. Your barrier is the variable.
Stratum corneum desquamation is disorganised. Often confused for 'glow'.

ingredients.name: Cholesterol
ingredients.evidence: Rebuilds the stratum-corneum bilayer alongside ceramides.

ingredients.name: Free fatty acids
ingredients.evidence: Linoleic acid; supports the third pillar of the bilayer.

ingredients.name: Panthenol 5%
ingredients.evidence: Pro-vitamin B5. Reduces TEWL within 48 hours.

ingredients.name: Niacinamide 4–5%
ingredients.evidence: Replicated upregulation of endogenous ceramide synthesis.

ingredients.name: Petrolatum
ingredients.evidence: Reduces TEWL by ~99%. The reference occlusive.

phases.b: Cleanser + ceramide moisturiser + SPF only. No actives, no acids, no retinoids, no fragrance.
Centella or panthenol serum AM + PM. Continue zero-active routine.
Niacinamide 4–5% in moisturiser only. Watch for sting; hold if any.
Twice-weekly retinoid OR azelaic — never both, never more than twice a week to start.
